note: Day 27 (Thursday). CAMPAIGN MILESTONE DAY. All previously active connection notes (leads 50/52/55/56 from Day 26 run) are now EXPIRED — their Day-14 breakup emails fire today (fu_046-049, 09:00-09:45). Same-day-as-breakup rule blocks all four. No new active notes. Post-breakup contingency instructions below for any Day 26 requests that are pending acceptance. LinkedIn note generation resumes with Batch 5 Ryedale leads.

strategy.tone: slightly awkward genuine compliment — stands out from polished AI outreach slop
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by'
Do not mention AI or automation
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity only
Do not use 'reach out' or 'touch base'
Do not mention 'digital marketing' or 'web design' explicitly

no_active_notes_reason: All remaining candidates (leads 50/52/55/56) are blocked today — breakup emails fire today (fu_046-049, 09:00-09:45). Same-day LinkedIn rule is absolute. No new leads in pipeline (Batch 5 Ryedale data not received).

post_breakup_contingency.applies_if: Any of the Day 26 LinkedIn requests (leads 52/55/50/56) were sent by HANDS yesterday (2026-04-23) but not yet accepted — and the lead accepts the connection AFTER today's breakup email
post_breakup_contingency.rule: If a lead accepts post-breakup: send the if_accepted_message from the archived note. Do NOT reference the breakup email. Do NOT mention the email sequence. Respond naturally as a new LinkedIn connection.
post_breakup_contingency.probability: LOW — but possible if requests were sent yesterday and accepted today or later

archived_notes.archive_reason: Breakup email fu_046 fires today at 09:00 — same-day LinkedIn rule. Note expired.
archived_notes.note: Linda — Holderness Homecare came up when I was looking at independent care providers around Beverley. Something about the online presence caught my attention. Connecting in case it's relevant.
archived_notes.if_accepted_message: Linda — thanks for connecting. I noticed something about the Holderness Homecare online presence worth a quick mention. Happy to share if useful.
archived_notes.post_breakup_contingency: If Linda accepts a LinkedIn request sent yesterday (2026-04-23): send if_accepted_message as the opening message. She may not have seen or connected the breakup email to this request.
archived_notes.upgrade_note_archived: If Linda posts about care quality, CQC inspections, staff recognition, or community volunteering: upgrade to post_reference using — 'Linda — your post about [X] stood out. Holderness Homecare came up when I was looking at independent care providers around Beverley. Something about the online presence worth mentioning. Connecting in case it's relevant.' (trim to 200 chars)

archived_notes.if_accepted_message: Neil — thanks for connecting. Noticed something about the East Riding Electrical website worth a quick mention. Happy to share if it's useful.

batch_5_note_templates.status: Ready for Batch 5 Ryedale leads. Note generation begins once initial emails have fired for 3+ days.
batch_5_note_templates.post_reference_template: [Name] — saw your post about [X]. [Business] caught my eye looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the site worth mentioning. Connecting in case it's useful.
batch_5_note_templates.generic_template: [Name] — [Business] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Something about the [website/online presence] caught my attention. Connecting in case it's relevant.
batch_5_note_templates.headline_template: [Name] — [headline reference] caught my attention. [Business] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful.
batch_5_note_templates.char_limit_reminder: All templates must trim to 200 chars before sending. Post_reference achieves 3-4x higher acceptance. Always check profile for recent posts before defaulting to generic.

sector_priority.rule: High-LinkedIn sectors: attempt all leads with full name. Low-LinkedIn sectors: only attempt if full name known AND profile found within 5 minutes of searching.

summary.campaign_linkedin_summary: Zero confirmed LinkedIn contacts in entire Batch 1-4 campaign (Batches 1-4, ~60 leads, 27 days). All notes remained generic due to absence of linkedin_profiles.json from HANDS across 7 BRAIN runs. The LinkedIn channel was architecturally ready and daily-planned — the operational gap was HANDS execution latency.
summary.key_learning_for_batch_5: The 'post_reference upgrade' step requires HANDS to check each profile BEFORE sending the note — this step was never executed. For Batch 5: HANDS must open LinkedIn, search, check for posts, then send within the same session. Do not split search and send across days.
summary.next_active_notes: Batch 5 Ryedale leads. Earliest: 2026-04-28 (Monday). Most likely: 2026-04-30 (Wednesday) or later.
